endpoints.@comment: Feature: Aggregation and basic transformation

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint fetches in parallel the user information and the repository information from Githuib and returns it together. Non interesting fields are removed.
documentation/openapi.summary: Merges two calls from github and serves under a single endpoint

documentation/postman.description: Fetches user info and repositories from GitHub API in parallel, merges results, and filters unnecessary fields
documentation/postman.name: Aggregate GitHub User Data

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Advanced transformation with query language

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint fetches some crypto capitalization data from Coingecko for a given currency.
documentation/openapi.summary: Get TOP 5 crypto capitalization & price data for a given currency

documentation/postman.description: Fetches top 5 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization from Coingecko, sorts them by name, and transforms the response using JMESPath query language
documentation/postman.name: Get TOP 5 Cryptocurrencies by Market Cap

modifier/jmespath.@comment: Get first 5 crypto by market capitalization, then reverse sort them by name, finally filter and rename some fields
modifier/jmespath.expr: reverse(sort_by(collection[:5], &name))[*].{name: name, token: symbol,price: current_price, price_change: price_change_percentage_24h, market_cap: market_cap}

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Regular expression manipulation

regexp.@comment: Ridiculous card masking. Show last 4 digits and remove the rest. Credit card number is nested.

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint fetches the user information and masks the credit card number
documentation/openapi.summary: Mask the credit card number

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates regex-based data masking by showing only the last 4 digits of a credit card number
documentation/postman.name: Mask Credit Card Number

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Backend cache

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates backend-level caching using Cache-Control headers to reduce expensive API calls to external services
documentation/openapi.summary: Backend HTTP cache for crypto market data

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates backend-level HTTP caching for expensive API calls (Bitcoin/Ethereum market data)
documentation/postman.name: Backend Cache Demo

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Mocked response

documentation/openapi.description: Returns predefined static JSON data without calling any backend service, useful for testing, prototyping, and development
documentation/openapi.summary: Static mocked response without backend

documentation/postman.description: Returns a static JSON response without calling any backend, useful for testing and prototyping
documentation/postman.name: Mocked/Static Response

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Sequential calls, using values from 1st call response into 2nd call request

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates sequential processing where the response from the first backend call is used to construct the second request (hotel lookup followed by destination details)
documentation/openapi.summary: Sequential backend calls with response chaining

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates sequential proxy pattern where the response from the first backend call is used in the second backend request
documentation/postman.name: Sequential Backend Calls

endpoints.@comment: Feature: A public endpoint that requires no authentication

documentation/openapi.description: Baseline example of a completely open endpoint requiring no authentication, useful for testing and comparison
documentation/openapi.summary: Public endpoint with no authentication

documentation/postman.description: Example of a completely public endpoint with no authentication requirements
documentation/postman.name: Public Endpoint (No Auth Required)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Authentication and authorization

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint offers a web interface to present auth/authz flow with Keycloack.
documentation/openapi.summary: Full example for JWT based authentication / authorization with Keycloack

documentation/postman.description: Interactive web interface demonstrating complete JWT authentication and authorization flow using Keycloak
documentation/postman.name: JWT Auth Demo Web Interface (Keycloak)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Authorization based on JWT (using Keycloak)

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint uses JWT authentication with Keycloak
documentation/openapi.summary: Some information behind JWT authentication protection (Keycloak)

documentation/postman.description: Protected endpoint requiring JWT token from Keycloak with 'moderator' role authorization
documentation/postman.name: JWT Auth with Role-Based Access (Keycloak)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Security policy engine for requests, responses and claim checking

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates security policy engine using CEL (Common Expression Language) to validate GDPR cookie acceptance before allowing tracking
documentation/openapi.summary: Cookie validation using CEL security policies

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates security policy engine using CEL expressions to validate cookies before allowing access
documentation/postman.name: Cookie-Based Access Control (CEL Policy)

error.body: User did not accept the cookie and tracking is not allowed

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Authorization based on JWT (using KrakenD JOSE signer)

documentation/openapi.description: Protected endpoint using KrakenD's built-in JWT signing and validation with role-based authorization (requires role_a or role_c)
documentation/openapi.summary: JWT authentication with KrakenD internal signer

documentation/postman.description: Protected endpoint using KrakenD's built-in JWT signer with role-based authorization (requires role_a or role_c)
documentation/postman.name: JWT Auth with Custom Signer (KrakenD JOSE)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: JWT signing, as described at https://www.krakend.io/docs/authorization/jwt-signing/

documentation/openapi.description: Wraps backend-provided tokens (access_token, refresh_token) in KrakenD-signed JWTs for enhanced security and validation
documentation/openapi.summary: JWT token signing for backend tokens

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates JWT signing by wrapping backend tokens in KrakenD-signed JWTs for enhanced security
documentation/postman.name: JWT Token Signing

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Authorization based on API Keys

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint uses API-Key authentication
documentation/openapi.summary: Some information behind API-Keys authentication protection

documentation/postman.description: Protected endpoint using API Key authentication with role-based authorization (requires 'admin' role)
documentation/postman.name: API Key Authentication (Enterprise)

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Wildcard to our fake API, protected with Basic Auth

extra_config.@comment: The simple inclusion of the Auth Basic component enables it for this endpoint

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint will send all requests to a designed backend including everything after the /fake-api-auth path, adding Basic Authentication
documentation/openapi.summary: Calls to internal service with wildcard, adding auth

documentation/postman.description: Wildcard endpoint that proxies all requests to internal fake API with Basic Authentication protection
documentation/postman.name: Basic Authentication with Wildcard Proxy

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Aggregation + backend rate limiting + endpoint rate limiting

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates both endpoint-level and backend-level rate limiting while aggregating multiple data sources (campaigns and products)
documentation/openapi.summary: Multi-level rate limiting with aggregation

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates endpoint-level and backend-level rate limiting with aggregation of multiple data sources
documentation/postman.name: Multi-Level Rate Limiting

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Concurrent calls - Not enabled

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint fetches crypto market information from Coingecko using a single non-concurrent call.
documentation/openapi.summary: Get crypto market information with a single call

documentation/postman.description: Fetches crypto market data with a single sequential call (baseline for comparison with concurrent endpoint)
documentation/postman.name: Sequential Backend Call (No Concurrency)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Concurrent calls - Enabled

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint fetches crypto market information from Coingecko using 3 concurrent calls.
documentation/openapi.summary: Get crypto market information with 3 concurrent calls

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates concurrent requests by making 3 parallel calls to the same backend endpoint for improved performance
documentation/postman.name: Concurrent Backend Calls (3x Parallelization)

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Only POST method allowed

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint only allows POST requests
documentation/openapi.summary: Only POST method allowed

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates method-specific routing by allowing only POST requests
documentation/postman.name: HTTP Method Restriction (POST Only)

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Web Server - Static content

documentation/openapi.description: Serves static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and image files from the demo directory for the playground demo website
documentation/openapi.summary: Static file server for demo website

documentation/postman.description: Serves static HTML/CSS/JS files from the demo directory for the playground demo website
documentation/postman.name: Serve Static Website Files

endpoints.@comment: Feature: Expose a GET REST endpoint from POST to SOAP service

backend/soap.@comment: The XML template with variables is in the following path:

documentation/openapi.description: Converts SOAP XML web service to REST JSON endpoint, fetching country capital information from a legacy SOAP service
documentation/openapi.summary: SOAP to REST conversion for country capital lookup

documentation/postman.description: Converts SOAP XML web service to REST JSON endpoint (country capital lookup)
documentation/postman.name: SOAP to REST Conversion

endpoints.@comment: Feature: GraphQL to REST

documentation/openapi.description: Converts GraphQL API to REST endpoint, fetching Star Wars movie information including species and homeworld data from SWAPI GraphQL
documentation/openapi.summary: GraphQL to REST conversion for Star Wars films

documentation/postman.description: Converts GraphQL query to REST endpoint (Star Wars films data from SWAPI)
documentation/postman.name: GraphQL to REST Conversion

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: GRPC

documentation/openapi.description: Aggregates multiple gRPC services (flights, trains, hotels) into a single REST endpoint with intelligent parameter mapping and protocol conversion
documentation/openapi.summary: gRPC to REST aggregation for travel search

documentation/postman.description: Aggregates multiple gRPC services (flights, trains, hotels) into a single REST endpoint with parameter mapping
documentation/postman.name: gRPC to REST Aggregation (Travel Search)

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: GRPC Payload in Body

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates gRPC integration with POST method, mapping request body and path parameters to gRPC fields for flight booking
documentation/openapi.summary: gRPC POST endpoint with request body mapping

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates gRPC integration with POST method using request body for flight booking
documentation/postman.name: gRPC POST with Request Body

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: WebSockets (chat example)

documentation/openapi.description: WebSocket endpoint for real-time bidirectional chat communication with configurable room parameter, connect/disconnect events, and message buffering
documentation/openapi.summary: WebSocket chat room connection

documentation/postman.description: WebSocket endpoint for real-time chat functionality with connect/disconnect events and message buffering
documentation/postman.name: WebSocket Chat Connection

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: WebSockets (chat example web interface)

documentation/openapi.description: Web-based user interface for testing and interacting with WebSocket chat functionality in the browser
documentation/openapi.summary: WebSocket chat web interface

documentation/postman.description: Web interface for testing WebSocket chat functionality
documentation/postman.name: WebSocket Chat Web Interface

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Wildcard to our fake API, open

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint will send all requests to a designed backend including everything after the /fake-api path
documentation/openapi.summary: Calls to internal service with wildcard

documentation/postman.description: Open wildcard endpoint that proxies all requests to internal fake API without restrictions
documentation/postman.name: Wildcard Proxy (Open)

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Wildcard with geofencing - Allow visits only from Spain

documentation/openapi.description: This endpoint will send all requests to a designed backend including everything after the /fake-api-geofence path, adding geofencing (allowing calls only from Spain)
documentation/openapi.summary: Calls to internal service with wildcard, using geofencing

documentation/postman.description: Wildcard endpoint with geofencing using CEL validation to allow access only from Spain
documentation/postman.name: Geofenced Wildcard Proxy (Spain Only)

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Dynamic routing based on headers (token claim and cookie also available)

backend.@comment: We will route to the backend using the content of the 'X-Route' header

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates dynamic URL path construction using the value of the X-Route header to determine backend routing destination
documentation/openapi.summary: Dynamic routing based on request header

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates dynamic URL path construction using the X-Route header value
documentation/postman.name: Dynamic Routing by Header

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: Dynamic routing based on query strings

backend.@comment: We will route to the backend using the content of the 'X-Route' header

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates dynamic URL path construction using the 'route' query string parameter to determine backend routing destination
documentation/openapi.summary: Dynamic routing based on query string

documentation/postman.description: Demonstrates dynamic URL path construction using the 'route' query string parameter
documentation/postman.name: Dynamic Routing by Query String

endpoints.@comment: EE only feature: MCP Server - Entrypoint for Model Context Protocol

documentation/openapi.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server exposing country information aggregation tools. Accepts MCP protocol requests and routes them to appropriate handlers that aggregate REST and GraphQL APIs.
documentation/openapi.summary: MCP Server entrypoint for country data tools

documentation/postman.description: Model Context Protocol server endpoint that exposes country data aggregation tools for AI/LLM integration
documentation/postman.name: MCP Server Entrypoint (AI Gateway)

endpoints.@comment: AI Integration: LLM Routing

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates header-based LLM routing using conditional backends. Send X-Model: gemini, openai, or omit for Anthropic fallback.
documentation/openapi.summary: Routes requests to different LLM providers based on X-Model header

documentation/postman.description: Route to Gemini, OpenAI, or Anthropic (fallback) based on X-Model header
documentation/postman.name: LLM Routing by Header

endpoints.@comment: AI Integration: Conditional LLM Routing

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates conditional routing by user role. Moderators get routed to OpenAI (premium), readers get Gemini (basic). Requires JWT authentication via Keycloak.
documentation/openapi.summary: Routes to different LLMs based on JWT role claims

documentation/postman.description: Moderator → OpenAI, Reader → Gemini based on JWT role
documentation/postman.name: Conditional LLM Routing by Role

endpoints.@comment: AI Integration: Token Quota Management

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates AI cost control with token quotas. Moderators get 10000 tokens/day, readers get 1000 tokens/day. Uses Redis for persistent quota tracking.
documentation/openapi.summary: Enforces token-based usage quotas per user for LLM requests

documentation/postman.description: LLM endpoint with per-user token quotas enforced via Redis
documentation/postman.name: Token Quota Management

endpoints.@comment: AI Integration: Deterministic Prompt Guardrail

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates deterministic prompt guardrails using JSON Schema regex validation. Rejects requests containing credit card numbers, passwords, secrets, or API keys before reaching the LLM.
documentation/openapi.summary: Blocks prompts containing sensitive data patterns using regex validation

documentation/postman.description: Pattern-based prompt validation blocking sensitive data
documentation/postman.name: Deterministic Prompt Guardrail

endpoints.@comment: AI Integration: Intelligent Prompt Guardrail

documentation/openapi.description: Demonstrates AI-based prompt guardrails using sequential proxy. First classifies the prompt with Prompt Guard 2 22M model, then only forwards to Gemini if classified as BENIGN. Blocks injection and jailbreak attempts with a 403 error.
documentation/openapi.summary: Classifies prompts with AI safety model before forwarding to LLM

documentation/postman.description: AI-based prompt classifier + Gemini LLM via sequential proxy
documentation/postman.name: Intelligent Prompt Guardrail

error.body: {"error":"Prompt blocked","message":"Your prompt was classified as potentially unsafe and has been blocked by the AI guardrail"}

servers.instructions: Provides unified country data by aggregating REST and GraphQL APIs. Use this to retrieve comprehensive information about countries including geography, demographics, and cultural data.
servers.name: country-data-aggregator
servers.title: Country Information Aggregator

tools.description: Retrieves comprehensive country data by aggregating multiple public APIs (REST + GraphQL). Returns unified information including capital, population, area, languages, currency, flag, and bordering countries.

country_code.description: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., ES for Spain, FR for France, US for United States)

tools.name: get_country_info
tools.title: Get Country Information

backend.@comment: REST Countries API - provides geography, population, borders, flags
GraphQL Countries API - provides currency, native name, emoji, languages

backend/graphql.query: query GetCountry($code: ID!) { country(code: $code) { name native capital currency emoji languages { code name } } }

modifier/jmespath.@comment: Simplified merge - take first element from REST array and combine with GraphQL
modifier/jmespath.expr: {country: rest_data.countries[0].name.common, native_name: graphql_data.native, capital: rest_data.countries[0].capital[0], population: rest_data.countries[0].population, area_km2: rest_data.countries[0].area, currency: graphql_data.currency, languages: graphql_data.languages[*].name, flag_emoji: graphql_data.emoji, borders: rest_data.countries[0].borders}

auth/basic.@comment: Location of the password file for endpoints requiring basic auth

documentation/postman.description: Comprehensive demonstration environment showcasing KrakenD Enterprise capabilities including API composition, authentication, protocol conversion, rate limiting, and AI integration via MCP.

folder.description: Endpoints demonstrating API aggregation, data transformation, masking, and mocked responses
folder.name: /API Composition & Transformation

folder.description: Various authentication methods: JWT (Keycloak), API Keys, Basic Auth, and token signing
folder.name: /Authentication & Authorization

folder.description: QoS features including multi-level rate limiting, backend caching, and concurrent requests
folder.name: /Rate Limiting & Caching

folder.description: Dynamic routing, wildcard endpoints, geofencing, and HTTP method restrictions
folder.name: /Routing & Wildcards

folder.description: Convert between protocols: REST to gRPC, GraphQL to REST, SOAP XML to REST JSON
folder.name: /Protocol Conversion

folder.description: SOAP XML to REST JSON conversion examples
folder.name: /Protocol Conversion/SOAP

folder.description: WebSocket connectivity and real-time chat functionality
folder.name: /WebSockets & Real-time

folder.description: Advanced security policy enforcement using CEL expressions
folder.name: /Security Policies

folder.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AI/LLM integration (Enterprise-only feature)
folder.name: /AI & MCP Server

folder.description: Serve static files and demo website
folder.name: /Static Content

documentation/postman.name: KrakenD Enterprise API Gateway - Playground
